title: Bulk report - Week 37
summary:
Capesize
The Capesize market experienced a mixed week, beginning with positive sentiment but gradually facing increasing pressure. It was a slow start, although optimism remained as the BCI 5TC rose by $400 to $28,232, driven by limited but stronger Pacific fixtures. However, cargo volume and market momentum began to wane, particularly in the Pacific, leading to a decline in both the C5 and C3 markets.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: The Capesize market experienced a mixed week, beginning with positive sentiment but gradually facing increasing pressure. It was a slow start, although optimism remained as the BCI 5TC rose by $400 to $28,232, driven by limited but stronger Pacific fixtures.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: A week for the optimists in the Panamax sector with steady rises throughout the Atlantic and Asian markets, although we seemed to have reach a period of consolidation as the week ended. From the Atlantic basin, we saw decent levels of both grain and mineral demand versus a limited tonnage list, creating the perfect storm for owners, reports of low $10,000s achieved for a trans-Atlantic trip via US Gulf delivery this side.

children.title: Ultramax/Supramax
children.summary: A rather varied week for the sector. The Atlantic could be described as patchy, whilst the US Gulf gained momentum with a stronger cargo flow, the South Atlantic lacked fresh impetus and downward pressure remained.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The Handysize market saw minimal visible activity across both basins this week. In the Continent and Mediterranean, the market fundamentals remained unchanged with sentiment appearing positional.
